## 3. Deep Research panel

**Lifetime DR funnel** — only 87 / 539 rows (16.1%) ever carried a DR verdict, but that's expected since DR only fires on BUY / BUY_LIMIT (n=129 lifetime). DR coverage of BUY/BUY_LIMIT = **86 / 129 = 66.7%** — meaning **43 BUY/BUY_LIMIT rows lifetime never received a DR verdict**.

| DR review verdict | Count | % of DR-touched |
|---|---:|---:|
| ADJUSTED | 48 | 55.2% |
| OVERRIDDEN | 32 | 36.8% |
| ERROR_PARSING | 3 | 3.4% |
| UPGRADED | 2 | 2.3% |
| CONFIRMED | 1 | 1.1% |
| INCOMPLETE_TRADING_LEVELS | 1 | 1.1% |

Where DR took the PM and what it did with it:

- **ADJUSTED (48)** — almost entirely tightens entries: 23 PM-BUY → DR-BUY_LIMIT, 13 BUY_LIMIT → BUY_LIMIT (level changes), 10 BUY → BUY, 1 BUY → WATCH, 1 BUY_LIMIT → BUY.
- **OVERRIDDEN (32)** — all kills: 25 BUY → AVOID and 7 BUY_LIMIT → AVOID. DR has so far overridden ~25% of every BUY/BUY_LIMIT that reached it.

**Today's DR cases (individual)** — 2 of the 3 BUY/BUY_LIMIT names processed; **SOLS still pending DR** at audit time:

| Ticker | PM verdict | DR verdict | DR action | DR conviction | One-line DR reasoning |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **INTU** | BUY | ADJUSTED | **BUY_LIMIT** | MODERATE | $8B buyback + 13× fwd P/E is a real valuation floor, but shrinking Mailchimp revenue + DIY share losses make this a structural repricing — trade only as mean-reversion with strict limits and wider stops. |
| **WMT** | BUY | ADJUSTED | **BUY_LIMIT** | LOW | Extreme CCI -294 supports a violent short bounce, but 44× P/E is being reset by Q2 guidance misses, sector contagion, and fuel-driven margin compression — full recovery to prior highs unlikely. |
| **SOLS** | BUY_LIMIT | *(none)* | — | — | DR not yet run at 15:48 ET audit snapshot — see HIGH flag in §6. |

Both DR-touched names today were *softened* (BUY → BUY_LIMIT), neither killed. Convictions are MODERATE and LOW — DR is not enthusiastic about either.

## 6. Broken-service / data-health flags

| Sev | Flag | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **🔴 HIGH** | **Desk-review worker has not run in ~6.85 days.** All 5 ACTIVE positions show `last_reviewed_at = 2026-05-14T19:35`. Current prices are stale; stop/TP checks cannot fire. | `SELECT last_reviewed_at FROM desk_positions WHERE status='ACTIVE'` → all 2026-05-14. |
| **🔴 HIGH** | **WTW position should be stopped out but is still ACTIVE.** Current 247.72 < stop 253.50; headroom -2.33%. Direct consequence of the stalled review worker. | `WTW: current 247.72, stop 253.50` |
| **🔴 HIGH** | **`decision_tracking` is completely empty (0 rows).** The 1-week price-tracking job has never written. Outcome accuracy of any past recommendation cannot be measured. | `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM decision_tracking → 0` |
| **🔴 HIGH** | **Deep Research did not process SOLS** (PM = BUY_LIMIT, screened ~15:38, audit snapshot ~15:48). With the 60s DR rate-limit and only 3 BUY/BUY_LIMIT candidates today, this should have completed. If still missing tomorrow, the DR worker queue is stuck. | `INTU & WMT got DR; SOLS did not` |
| **🟠 MEDIUM** | **WMT shows `decision_points.status='Owned'` but has no ACTIVE row in `desk_positions`.** Either an entry-fill writer succeeded on `decision_points` but failed on `desk_positions`, or the desk_position is being created on a different cycle than today's. Possible duplicate-write or partial-write bug. | `dp.status='Owned' for id=542 WMT; no desk_positions.decision_point_id=542` |
| **🟠 MEDIUM** | **104 of 108 `Owned` `decision_points` have no matching ACTIVE `desk_position`.** 40 line up with CLOSED desk_positions but the dp.status was never flipped to 'Closed'. 64 have *no* desk_position record at all. The two tables are out of sync. | See queries in body. |
| **🟠 MEDIUM** | **`ai_score` is NULL on 529 / 539 lifetime rows** (98%), including all 10 today. The score column is effectively dead — either the producer was removed or never wired up. | `SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE ai_score IS NULL → 529` |
| **🟠 MEDIUM** | **`sector` is NULL on 531 / 539 rows** (98.5%); only WMT today carries a value (Consumer Staples). Sector-aware reporting and clustering can't work. | `SELECT sector, COUNT(*) FROM decision_points GROUP BY sector` |
| **🟠 MEDIUM** | **`subscribers` table is empty.** The daily email summary has no recipients — the background email task is running but delivering to nobody. | `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscribers → 0` |
| **🟠 MEDIUM** | **DR coverage gap on BUY/BUY_LIMIT historically: 43 / 129 = 33%** lifetime BUY/BUY_LIMIT rows never received a DR verdict. Some of those are likely the very-early rows before DR existed, but the rate is high enough to warrant a sweep. | `SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE recommendation IN ('BUY','BUY_LIMIT') AND deep_research_review_verdict IS NULL → 43` |
| **🟠 MEDIUM** | **`batch_comparisons` last write was 2026-05-08** — the batch-comparison feature appears dormant for ~13 days even though BUY/BUY_LIMIT candidates have continued to arrive. | `SELECT MAX(date) FROM batch_comparisons → 2026-05-08` |
| **🟡 LOW** | **3 ERROR_PARSING DR verdicts lifetime** (3.4% of DR-touched). Low absolute count but worth periodic spot-checks of the DR output schema. | `deep_research_review_verdict='ERROR_PARSING'` count = 3 |
| **🟡 LOW** | **`status` field uses string sentinels ('Not Owned' / 'Owned')** with no values for stuck/pending/closed states. The audit looked for `status='PENDING'` per spec and found none — that check is structurally meaningless with the current schema. | All 539 rows have status in {'Not Owned','Owned'}. |
| **🟡 LOW** | Today's volume (10) is below the trailing 30-day mean (13.7) and well below Mon–Wed (22–29). Not a fault — likely calmer tape — but worth watching for two more sessions. | See §1 daily volume. |

---

## 7. Insights & takeaways

- **The desk-review worker has been silently down for nearly a week.** That single failure cascades into the WTW phantom, stale current_price across all five active positions, and an inability to fire stop or trailing-stop exits. Fixing this is by far the highest-impact action.
- **DR continues to behave like a sensible gatekeeper.** Lifetime ~25% of every BUY/BUY_LIMIT it sees gets killed (32 OVERRIDDEN → AVOID) and most of the rest get tightened to BUY_LIMIT entries (48 ADJUSTED). Today's two cases (INTU, WMT) followed the modal pattern — softened to BUY_LIMIT with caveats. This is exactly the conservative bias the design intended.
- **The book is profitable but driven entirely by TAKE_PROFIT.** 15 TP exits contributed +94 pts; DESK_DECISION exits net -6.5 pts; STOP_LOSS / TRAILING_STOP cost ~-43 pts. The PM/DR are picking real bounces, but the "manual" desk_decision exits give back nearly everything they save — a fair amount of the realized P&L gain is being self-inflicted.
- **`decision_tracking` being empty means none of the above can be evidence-graded.** The "feed past accuracy into the PM prompt" item in the backlog cannot start until tracking actually writes rows.
- **`ai_score` and `sector` are effectively dead columns.** Either reinstate the producers or drop them from prompts and dashboards — partial schemas confuse readers and downstream agents.
- **WMT was opened today against a LOW-conviction DR** (44× P/E being structurally reset, no return to highs). The position is consistent with the BUY_LIMIT entry band (current ~121.69 vs entry band 115.50–119.50, so technically it overshot — worth confirming entry was at the limit, not market).
- **Today's mix is unusually risk-off: 40% AVOID, only 30% actionable.** DEEP_DIP gatekeeper tier dominated (8 of 10), consistent with a tape that had a few real-news drops, not noise. Small sample, but worth flagging if it repeats tomorrow.
- **No new subscribers in lifetime data.** If outbound email is part of the value loop, the funnel into `subscribers` is not connected.

---

## 8. Suggested next actions

Prioritised by impact:

1. **🔴 Restart / debug the desk-review worker.** Check the daemon thread that updates `desk_positions.current_price`, `unrealized_pnl_pct`, `last_reviewed_at` and fires exit conditions. All five active positions have a 2026-05-14 timestamp — pull worker logs from that window.
2. **🔴 Manually reconcile WTW.** It's already below its stop. Either flip it to CLOSED with `STOP_LOSS` and the right exit_price, or — if you decide to keep it — explicitly widen the stop and document the override.
3. **🔴 Investigate why `decision_tracking` has 0 rows.** Confirm whether the tracking task is enabled in `main.py` background tasks and whether it's silently swallowing exceptions. This is the single biggest gap for any future backtest/feedback work.
4. **🟠 Run the DR worker against SOLS** (id=541). If it fails to process within 5 min on a normal-load day, restart the DR worker thread and inspect its dual-queue state.
5. **🟠 One-off reconciliation script** to bring `decision_points.status` in line with `desk_positions.status`: flip 40 rows from `Owned` → `Closed` and resolve the 64 orphan `Owned` rows.
6. **🟠 Decide the fate of `ai_score` and `sector`.** If still needed, restore the producers (sector from Polygon/Finnhub; ai_score from sensor average). If not, drop from the schema and any prompts that read them.
7. **🟠 Resume `batch_comparisons`.** Last run 2026-05-08; today had 3 BUY/BUY_LIMIT candidates — exactly the situation it was designed for.
8. **🟠 Fix the scheduled-task delivery paths.** This audit was forced into the fallback because the two primary folders aren't in the session's connected folders. Either mount those two folders into the Cowork session or change the task's destinations.
9. **🟡 Sweep the 43 lifetime BUY/BUY_LIMIT rows missing DR**, run DR retroactively where reasonable, and confirm whether they correspond to the pre-DR era or to genuine misses.
10. **🟡 Track downside-recapture on DESK_DECISION exits.** 18 such exits net slightly negative; worth a short investigation into whether they're justified by realized drawdown or whether they exit winners prematurely.
